signals.reasoning: BTC is still recovering, but with macro now at RED 42 and no unique edge versus the alt squeeze names, it remains context rather than a fresh trade.
ETH is constructive, but the move still looks like broad recovery rather than a standout fresh setup. Neutral is cleaner than forcing it.
SOL has recovered enough to look better, but the move is already well underway and does not offer a cleaner edge than the best squeeze candidate.
TST is simply too extended after the rebound. Even with squeeze characteristics, the reward-to-risk is worse now than it was an hour ago.

STABLE still has some squeeze fuel in predicted funding, but the live structure has become more mixed and current funding is no longer strongly supportive enough to justify a fresh long under weaker macro.
WCT continues to trend well, but it is already extended and too messy on execution quality to treat as a clean new entry.
ZEREBRO still reads like a short-covering rebound rather than a durable fresh long. High beta after the first recovery leg makes it too easy to get trapped.
STBL has recovered, but positive funding and already-improved structure make it an awkward fresh entry. It is no longer the kind of dislocation worth chasing.
BOME is still too thin and noisy to trust for a clean trade. Fast movement alone is not enough.
JTO remains the cleanest reduced-size long because negative funding is still materially supportive and execution quality is better than the smaller names. Macro is weaker, so confidence stays capped.
VVV still looks more like a short-covering rebound than a clean fresh trend continuation. It is not broken enough to short and not clean enough to buy.
